author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Pali Canon - Udana 1974
content:
UDANA SEMINAR 1974

CONTENTS
INTRODUCTION
Pali and other Canons, composition thereof
The Udana
CHAPTER ONE: ENLIGHTENMENT
Sutta I
Sitting for seven days
Rebirth and anatta
Use of words: "Brahmin" and "Buddha"
Sutta ii
Watchs of the night and imagery of the Sun
Collections of the teachings; meditation and study
Sutta iv
Vedas
"brahma", "Tathagatha"
Sutta V
'What things constitute a Brahmin?'
Hierarchy of disciples?
Sutta vi
Access to magical realms
Anannaposin
Sutta vii
Yakkha and "spirit" worship
Sutta viii
Differences in ancient and Western views on discarding
family responsibility
celibacy and "relationships"
Sutta ix
Sutta x
Mindfulness
Burial mounds
General discussion on Chapter One
Ancient Greece and India Cultural context in which
Buddha communicated his experience.
CHAPTER TWO: MUCALINDA
Sutta I
Chakras and centres and stupa meditation
Sutta ii
Kingdoms of Nagadha and Kosala
'Not eating after midday'
Loss of mindfulness
Sutta iii
Copying mannerisms; attraction to differing 'wavelengths' Sutta iv
Wanderers
Personal responsibility for having a body
Pleasure and pain in higher mental states
Four types of person in spiritual evolution
Sutta v
Sutta vi
Sutta vii
Suffering on account of bereavement
"contact" after death
Sutta Viii
Loss of mindfulness: pleasure/pain
"Relationships" attachment
Sutta ix
Keeping the Buddha to the right side
Sutta x
Rich people; sleep
General discussion on Chapter Two
collators of the Udana
CHAPTER THREE: NANDA
Sutta i

(DAYS 3and 4 NOT RECORDED)


CHAPTER FIVE: THE ELDER SONA
Sutta i
"Let the selflover harm no other man
Sutta
Early instance of Bodhisattva principle
Tushita heaven and monks; urinals
Sutta iii
Systematization of doctrinal terms; Pacceka Buddhas
Dukkha and escaping
Curing diseases
Sutta iv
Maliciousness in children
Sutta v
Full Moons and Pratimoksha
The image of the "mighty ocean"
Limbs of Enlightenment
Once Returners and the Pure Abodes
Sutta vi
Informality when meeting the Buddha
Buddhist names
Translations of the Sutta Nipata
Sutta vii
Musing thought Contemplation
"Old" Buddhists in Germany
Sutta viii
Schism
Sutta ix
Definition of 'neurotic'
Sutta x
NB: Musing thought page ll


(DAYS 6 and 7 RECORDINGS LOST)
